what_it_does: Documents a design decision that clone:// (fleet/provenance session identity) and mind:// (composable thinking-unit) are separate address axes that compose through a shared grammar, rather than being collapsed into one scheme.
observations: clone:// is defined as fleet/provenance identity: (source-sid × cut-point × era)
mind:// is defined as composable thinking-unit: (model × role × cognition)
A live clone is driven by a mind, but the two addresses remain at different granularities on different axes
connect-and-unify means one grammar plus composable references, not a merged address scheme
Collapsing clone≡mind is explicitly forbidden by R13 weights and Tim's reuse-don't-parallel principle
Provenance cites Tim's 'a and b, connect and unify', fork's separate-axes answer, and advisor confirmation
Anchors two live builds: fork's clone:// fleet-addressing and fork's R13 composable-mind
Related to a shared contracts.address grammar and fleet-law board://item-e4296b31
standouts: Orthogonality of clone:// and mind:// is the central claim
Explicit prohibition against collapsing the two axes into one address
Lattice-grounded reasoning reused from a prior addressing decision
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, timestamps, and history
Markdown body with bullet list and a provenance section
Uses board://, clone://, and mind:// address notation inline
References other items by exact board id
concerns: Heavy jargon and named entities (R13, fork, Tim, fleet-law) are used without in-file definitions
No concrete example showing how a clone-row references a mind or vice versa
Phrases like 'R13 weights (clone-f1ade750)' and 'era-clones' onboarding reflections' are unexplained
notes: A guide item drawing a hard architectural line between session-clone identity and thinking-unit composition, insisting they stay separate but composable.
questions: What is R13 in this context?
Who or what is 'fork' and what machinery does it own?
What does the fleet-law board://item-e4296b31 actually prescribe?
How is the shared contracts.address grammar implemented?
What is the 'multi-space lattice' referenced as grounding?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a guide explaining why clone and mind addresses are orthogonal and composable.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to prevent a category error where fleet/provenance identity and thinking-unit composition are mistaken for the same thing, and to secure a shared grammar that lets each axis reference the other.
novelty: It treats session clones and model/role minds as orthogonal address axes in a lattice and explicitly rejects merging them into a single address scheme.
contribution: It contributes the principle that clone provenance and mind composition should remain distinct address axes while sharing a composable reference grammar, preserving both fleet-identity and thinking-unit granularity.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard guide asserting that clone:// (fleet/provenance session identity) and mind:// (composable thinking-unit) are orthogonal address axes that compose through a shared grammar rather than being collapsed into a single scheme.
intention_for_embedding: To settle a design granularity question by declaring clone and mind addresses separate-but-composable and to anchor two live builds under a unified address grammar.
